cards.name: Vesica Piscis
cards.description: The almond shape formed where two circles overlap — the space that belongs to both and neither.
cards.text: Where do your intentions for today and the group's needs actually overlap, and what lives in that shared sliver?

cards.name: Catenary
cards.description: The curve a chain makes when it hangs freely between two points — strongest under its own weight.
cards.text: Notice what in the room is holding itself up by gravity alone. Let the shape of that tension be information, not a problem to solve.

cards.name: Voronoi
cards.description: A pattern of cells where every point inside a cell is closest to one seed — boundaries drawn by proximity alone.
cards.text: Whose presence are you closest to right now, and whose territory are you standing inside without meaning to?

cards.name: Torus
cards.description: A donut-shaped surface — the path of energy that returns to itself through its own centre.
cards.text: The wound is the place where the Light enters you.

cards.name: Geodesic
cards.description: The shortest path between two points along a curved surface — a straight line that knows the world is round.
cards.text: What is the most honest route between where the group is now and where it wants to be — even if it doesn't look like a straight line?

cards.name: Penrose Tiling
cards.description: A non-repeating pattern that covers the plane without ever quite repeating itself — ordered but never regular.
cards.text: Consider what in your practice looks like a pattern but has never actually happened the same way twice.

cards.name: Ogive
cards.description: The pointed arch that carries weight by splitting it sideways — a threshold that holds by leaning.
cards.text: What are you leaning on today, and what is leaning back on you without you noticing?

cards.name: Lemniscate
cards.description: The figure-eight of infinity — a loop that crosses itself at the middle and keeps going.
cards.text: What you seek is seeking you.

cards.name: Frustum
cards.description: A cone or pyramid with its top cut off — a shape that points toward something it will never fully become.
cards.text: What in your life or work is pointing toward a summit you may never arrive at, and how does that change what you do with the base?

cards.name: Klein Bottle
cards.description: A surface with no inside and no outside — a container that passes through itself to become its own exterior.
cards.text: Sit with the parts of yourself you try to keep private and the parts you offer publicly. Notice where the seam has already dissolved.

cards.name: Reuleaux Triangle
cards.description: A shape of constant width that isn't a circle — it rolls smoothly but never has a centre in the usual sense.
cards.text: Where are you rolling steadily without a centre to return to, and is that freedom, avoidance, or both?

cards.name: Asymptote
cards.description: A line a curve approaches forever but never touches — intimacy without arrival.
cards.text: Bring to mind a relationship or goal you are perpetually approaching. Ask what the approach itself is giving you.

cards.name: Sierpinski Triangle
cards.description: A triangle made of smaller triangles made of smaller triangles — the same shape at every scale.
cards.text: What pattern keeps showing up in your life at every scale — in a breath, in a day, in a year?

cards.name: Caustic
cards.description: The bright curve of light refracted through a curved surface — beauty made from what distorts.
cards.text: There is a crack in everything. That's how the light gets in.

cards.name: Wabi-Sabi Crack
cards.description: A fault line honoured rather than hidden — the Japanese aesthetic of imperfect, incomplete, transient things.
cards.text: Notice one thing in yourself today that you have been trying to fix. Let it be evidence of having been used.

cards.name: Möbius Strip
cards.description: A loop with only one side — travel far enough and you meet yourself upside-down.
cards.text: Where are you and the person you're working with on the same side of a problem without realising it yet?

cards.name: Golden Spiral
cards.description: A spiral whose growth rate is the golden ratio — each turn wider, but in proportion to the last.
cards.text: And the day came when the risk to remain tight in a bud was more painful than the risk it took to blossom.

cards.name: Tangent Point
cards.description: The single point where a line just touches a curve without crossing it — contact without entry.
cards.text: Where in the group today might the right move be to touch lightly and not cross over?

cards.name: Hyperbolic Plane
cards.description: A surface that curves away from itself in every direction — there is always more room than you expected.
cards.text: Give yourself permission to take up more space than the room seems to offer. Notice what happens when you do.

cards.name: Ensō
cards.description: A circle drawn in a single brushstroke — whole precisely because it is not closed.
cards.text: From the withered tree, a flower blooms.
